topic: tank method and primary fruit retention
trigger_keywords: retencion de frescura y expresion frutal

steps.label: cause
steps.text: The second fermentation takes place in a sealed, pressure-resistant tank rather than in each final bottle.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Temperature-controlled stainless steel and relatively short lees contact limit oxidation and autolytic development while retaining carbon dioxide under pressure.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The finished sparkling wine preserves fresh primary fruit and floral aromas and can be produced faster and at lower cost.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The finished sparkling wine preserves fresh primary fruit and floral aromas and can be produced faster and at lower cost.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
